title: Technical Support Engineer (EMEA)
description: About Us Metronome is the leading usage\-based billing platform built for modern software companies. With Metronome, companies can launch products faster, offer any pricing model, and streamline finance workflows without writing code. Our platform computes millions of invoices per billing period and is scaling rapidly to accommodate new customers, saving them hours of development time and manual invoicing and enabling them to use consumption data to better serve their customers. Our customers love our product and approach, and we’re humbled to work with amazing companies like OpenAI, NVIDIA, Confluent, and Anthropic. You'll be joining an experienced team that includes founders who have successfully built and sold startups before. Our founders and employees also have direct experience building and scaling teams through massive growth at companies like Dropbox, Clever, and New Relic. On the back of this experience and our success\-to\-date, we’ve raised over $128M from leading investors including NEA, Andreessen Horowitz, General Catalyst, Elad Gil, and Workday Ventures. We’re also proud to have founders and executives of companies like Segment, Plaid, Looker, Gitlab, Confluent, HashiCorp, and Snowflake, as investors who have experienced the pain we're solving firsthand. About the Role As a member of our technical support engineering team, you will be on the front lines providing world\-class customer service. As part of our engineering organization, you will become an expert on our product and partner closely with Metronome's engineers, customer success, solutions architecture, and growth teams, as well as our customers' developers. Your primary responsibilities will include handling customer escalations through our ticketing system, using internal observability tools to diagnose and scope customer\-facing issues, collaborating directly with customers via Slack and other channels, and developing internal tools and documentation to improve the support experience. Since we view every support escalation as an opportunity to learn, both as individuals and as a company, you will play a critical role in providing feedback to our product team on common challenges and influencing our product roadmap. What You'll Do * Provide technical guidance to customers’ developers on best practices for integrating with our APIs and utilizing our functionality. * Troubleshoot any issues that arise from customers and diagnose any blockers. Escalate issues with customer context to the engineering team. * Develop internal tools to automate customer workflows that are currently unavailable in our product. Provide feedback on recurring patterns and advocate for addressing feature gaps in the product. * Design and assist in implementing a comprehensive knowledge base to assist customers with using our product. Impact You'll Have * Through your interactions with individual customers, you will help improve our product for everyone. * Your work will play a crucial role in ensuring our customers' success and the continued effectiveness of their integration with Metronome. * The Metronome team is more well\-rounded because you bring your unique personality to work and help create an inclusive fun environment focused on helping our customers get the most value out of Metronome. **Qualifications** * 2\+ years of work experience in a B2B customer\-facing technical support organization or in engineering * Experience working closely with engineering teams and providing technical feedback on customer issues * Ability to debug and triage bugs and escalations from customers * Can communicate technical capabilities of the product to customers * Experience writing scripts or internal tools using APIs, and functional knowledge of SQL * Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to break down complex technical concepts clearly * A mindset of customer empathy and ability to solve challenging problems Bonus points * Experience as an early or founding member of a support team, including building processes from scratch * Startup experience and familiarity with scaling a support team * Programming experience in one or more of Typescript, Python, or Ruby * You have worked on modern enterprise software which is business critical * Experience creating knowledge base articles and internal documentation * Familiarity with at least one ticketing system, such as Pylon, Zendesk, Front, or similar *We're an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status.*

title: Technical Support Specialist – eCommerce / SaaS
description: **Welcome to the Agentic Commerce Era** At Commerce, our mission is to empower businesses to innovate, grow, and thrive with our open, AI\-driven commerce ecosystem. As the parent company of BigCommerce, Feedonomics, and Makeswift, we connect the tools and systems that power growth, enabling businesses to unlock the full potential of their data, deliver seamless and personalized experiences across every channel, and adapt swiftly to an ever\-changing market. Simply said, we help businesses confidently solve complex commerce challenges so they can build smarter, adapt faster, and grow on their own terms. If you want to be part of a team of bold builders, sharp thinkers, and technical trailblazers, working together to shape the future of commerce, this is the place for you. **What You Will Do** * Provide outstanding phone, email, and chat\-based technical support to our customers in a fast\-paced 24x7 environment (expect up to 7\.5 hours of talk/chat time a day). * Provide extraordinary support to our ever\-growing merchant base with your unsurpassed customer service and problem solving capabilities. * Develop solutions and utilize standard operating procedures for improving customer satisfaction and creating lifelong promoters of our brand. * Communicate with the management team and developers to improve product functionality and resolve issues. * Decrease incoming volume by striving toward first contact resolution and proactively onboarding and advising customers on eCommerce best practices. * Assist customers with common billing, invoice, and account issues. * Utilize classroom, video, and self\-paced training to stay ahead of product advancements and eCommerce best practices; utilize your knowledge to solve problems efficiently. * Display high levels of professionalism in recurring interactions with departments inside and outside of the CSS organization. **Who You Are** * A work ethic that demonstrates dedication to the company, its mission, and the team; a desire to work in a culture of excellence. * Experience in a customer\-facing, service\-oriented role is required; experience in a phone, email, or chat\-based technical support or customer service contact center preferred. * Exceptional ability to utilize self\-service resources (internal guides, Google\-fu, external service portals, etc) to find solutions is required. * Strong aptitude toward various web, eCommerce, and hosting technologies and how they work together. * Exceptional critical thinking and soft skills with a passion for getting every customer on the path to success using thoughtful solutions that best fit each situation. * Team player interested in growing and competing alongside some of the best technical support agents in the business; willingness to help and seek help from peers. * Exceptional written and verbal communication skills; an ability to communicate effectively with and deescalate frustrated or upset customers without taking it personally. * Ability to read or edit within at least one web language (HTML, CSS, Javascript, Jquery, etc), as well as a basic understanding of data structures, is preferred * Must be flexible to shift work and occasional overtime in a 24x7x365 environment. **Inclusion and Belonging** At Commerce, we believe that celebrating the unique histories, perspectives and abilities of every employee makes a difference for our company, our customers and our community. We are an equal opportunity employer and the inclusive atmosphere we build together will make room for every person to contribute, grow and thrive. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the interview process, to perform essential job functions and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. If you need an accommodation in order to interview at Commerce, please let us know during any of your interactions with our recruiting team. description: **Req ID:** 368242 NTT DATA strives to hire exceptional, innovative and passionate individuals who want to grow with us. title: Trainee Buyer - Construction
description: **Company Overview:** Established in 1925, PJ Hegarty is Ireland's most progressive building company. We undertake projects throughout Ireland and the UK from offices in Dublin, Cork, Limerick \& London and are a leading company in large scale construction with over 450 staff members, working together to deliver world class construction projects. **Why us?** Embarking on a career in procurement and supply chain management in 2026 is a promising move. It is a highly practical field where hands\-on experience often outweighs formal education, making it accessible to beginners. We are seeking to find and develop the next generation of purchasing talent within our purchasing department – you will be learning from the best **Key Duties:** * Procurement Support: Assist in sourcing, purchasing, and negotiating for construction materials, services and equipment. * Supplier Liaison: Learn how to build positive relationships with suppliers to secure competitive pricing, handle inquiries, and ensure on\-time delivery. * Administrative Accuracy: Maintain accurate records in procurement systems, process requisitions from site, purchase orders, and manage documentation. * Site Coordination: Work with site managers to ensure project material requirements are met within budget and on time. * Record Keeping: Assist with invoice reconciliation, supplier discrepancies, and data entry. * Participate in the quarterly review of the supply chain performance. **Requirements:** * Are organised, smart, energetic, enthusiastic person with attention to detail when performing tasks and a can\-do attitude. * Flexible in approach, a multi\-tasker with good administration and time management skills. * An effective communicator with sensible commercial acumen. * Have an ability to anticipate and manage problems, and ability to prioritise and escalate where necessary. * If you feel you are suited to the role as outlined, and that you can grow to meet the responsibilities as outlined please apply. * This is an entry level role, while some relevant experience would of course be an advantage, it is not strictly necessary. * Similarly, relevant third level education, completed or in progress would be an advantage but is not strictly necessary. **Further Information:** The company assists in career progression of its employees. CPD \& training will be discussed on an ongoing basis with the individual. **Diversity and Inclusion** The Company is an Equal Opportunities employer and recognises its responsibility to provide equality of opportunity to employees and prospective employees. Our Aim is to ensure that no job applicant or employee receives less favourable treatment on the grounds of religious belief, political opinion, gender, marital status, civil status, sexual orientation, disability, race, age and membership of the Traveller community. The Company will not accept or tolerate any form of discrimination, direct or indirect, by any member of the Company. The Company is committed to the fair treatment of employees at all times.

title: Transformation Engineer- CRH International
description: **Country:** United Kingdom **Req ID:** 523886 **Job Type** : Full Time **Workplace Type** : Hybrid **Seniority Level** : Mid\-Senior Level **About CRH** We are CRH, and we are committed to contributing to a more resilient and sustainable built environment. We understand the wider impact our businesses can make in supporting human activity. We continue to do this through the delivery of unique, superior building materials and products for use in road and critical utility infrastructure, commercial building projects and outdoor living solutions. CRH (NYSE: CRH, LSE: CRH) is the leading provider of building materials solutions that build, connect and improve our world. Employing c.78,500 people at c.3,390 operating locations in 28 countries, CRH has market leadership positions in both North America and Europe. As the essential partner for transportation and critical utility infrastructure projects, complex non\-residential construction and outdoor living solutions, CRH’s unique offering of materials, products and value\-added services helps to deliver a more resilient and sustainable built environment. The company is ranked among sector leaders by Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) rating agencies. A Fortune 500 company, CRH’s shares are listed on the NYSE and LSE. Without you noticing our products, we are everywhere you live, work, and relax. Our ***project portfolio*** includes some of the most sustainable and cutting\-edge building projects around the world. Think of the asphalt on the ***Silverstone Grand Prix Circuit, the Paris Metro Rail project, but also the Louis Vuitton Museum in Paris, parts of the Burj Khalifa, and the Kennedy Space Centre*** . Learn more about us through the following Link. **Position Overview** We have a list of operating companies that currently have independent technology services and full IT Teams. We have a programme to centralise those services and migrate the local IT services onto the central technology platforms known collectively as 'UnITy'. The UnITy migration process is technology focused. The technical stages include security tasks to ensure a minimum security posture prior to onboarding, migration plan per server, execution and decommission. These stages will involve the Transformation Engineer working with the operating company IT teams and infrastructure colleagues to complete full UnITy migration. Migration activities are driven by the internal centralization programme as well as new company acquisitions. **Key Tasks And Responsibilities** In this role, you will: * Work with the Transformation Lead and technology colleagues to define and plan the migration of local technical services to the centralized platforms and service offerings * Analyse the source environment and assist the OpCo directly with the planning and execution of remediation tasks – i.e. implementation steps, risk appreciation \& mitigation, etc. * Assist migration project managers in identifying and planning technical tasks as well as identifying risks and issues that may impact the project * Partake in risk management reviews and advise on mitigation plans * Contribute to technical migration plans and task lists in association with project managers and technical colleagues * Coordinate technical tasks between operating companies’ technical teams, central IT colleagues and relevant third party suppliers * Support users during migration activities to ensure smooth transitions when moving from local to centralized services * Contribute to specific communications to business teams regarding migration steps and business responsibilities **Individual Competencies** **You Are Able To** * Drive Results \- Consistently achieving objectives, even under tough circumstances, pushing self and others to accomplish goals * Have Courage \- Stepping up to address difficult issues, saying what needs to be said. * Have a Strategic Mindset \- Seeing ahead to future possibilities and translating them into breakthrough strategies * Be Resilient \- Rebounding from setbacks and adversity when facing difficult situations. * Collaborate \- Building partnerships and working collaboratively with others to meet shared objectives * Make Quality Decisions \- Making good and timely decisions that keep the organisation moving forward **Key Characteristics** **For This Role You Should Be/have** * Self\-starting and independent professional with strong collaboration skills * Ability to be outspoken about risks, issues and call out when steps have been missed or where improvements / amendments to the migration process are needed * Ability to work with external technicians and third parties with respect, harmony and honesty * Prepared to engage new team members, stakeholders and external collaborators to build strong, trust based relationships * Remains calm while managing difficult situations or technical issues when they arise * Respects deadlines and raises risks to timelines early * Customer focused including challenging processes or works that do not add value * Stays motivated and has a level of resilience in the face of setbacks **Key Functional Competencies** **You Possess The Following** * Technically competent on the following: * Networking – VLAN’s, routing, IP configuration, LAN/WAN topologies * Deep knowledge of Active Directory – Object structure and manipulation, DNS, DHCP, GPO’s, PKI, Powershell, authentication * Windows client operating systems * Firewall configuration and rule set construction * Understands ITIL concepts, such as Incident, Request \& Change * Competent in Virtual technologies such as VMware, Hyper\-V or Azure * An appreciation of environments with more stringent security, compliance, and process requirements * Able to analyse servers and applications and plan required migration changes * Technical knowledge and independent nature sufficient to analyse workloads, predict risks and issues, raise concerns and come up with solutions in a timely manner to avoid project delays while proactively working to avoid issues * Excellent documentation and record keeping * Sensitive to stakeholder management requirements and able to effectively communicate to different stakeholders including internal colleagues, operating companies’ stakeholders and third parties * Able to coordinate technical activities across technical teams * Language: English (Fluent). Desirable: Second European Language (preferred) * Able to translate / explain technical concepts to non\-technical audiences * Established organisational and time management skills, with the ability to handle multiple priorities. **Experience / Education** You are/have: * Educated to a bachelor’s degree level in a technical related subject or commensurate experience in the field * At least 5 years of technical / data centre migration experience * Demonstrable experience in assisting technical migration tasks in complex environments **What CRH Offers You** * A culture that values opportunity for growth, development, and internal promotion * Highly competitive salary package * Comprehensive secondary benefits * Significant contribution to your pension plan * Health and wellness programs * Excellent opportunities to develop and progress with a global organization **Connect your future to CRH** We are curious to learn more about you. At CRH, we believe our mutual differences contribute to the healthy, productive, and enjoyable workspace we create. Please introduce yourself and send us your application. Is this role not for you, but do you know someone who would love to join the team? Please let us know! CRH finds it important that vacancies are shared to individuals that may find them interesting and/or could be suitable for the role Please contact our recruitment team at careers@crh.com. *CRH is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to creating an inclusive work environment for all employees and actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community.* *Benefits/perks listed above may vary depending on the nature of the employment with CRH and the country where you work.* ***Please note that we cannot accept any applications submitted through email for GDPR purposes. Candidates must apply through our job portal.*** ***We do not accept candidate introductions for this position from recruitment agencies, unless you have been instructed to do so by our recruitment team.***

title: Treasury Analyst - Dublin
description: **Country:** Ireland **City:** Dublin **Req ID:** 522998 **Job Type** : Full Time **Workplace Type** : Onsite **Seniority Level** : Associate **About CRH** CRH is the leading provider of building materials critical to modernizing infrastructure. with out team of 83,000 people across 4,000 locations, our unmatched scale, connected portfolio, and deep local relationships make us the partner of choice for transportation, water, and reindustrialization projects, shaping communities for a better tomorrow. CRH (NYSE: CRH) is a member of the S\&P 500 Index. For more information, visit www.crh.com. Without you noticing our products, we are everywhere you live, work, and relax. Our project portfolio includes some of the most sustainable and cutting\-edge building projects around the world. Think of the asphalt on the Silverstone Grand Prix Circuit, the Paris Metro Rail project, but also the Louis Vuitton Museum in Paris, parts of the Burj Khalifa, and the Kennedy Space Centre. CRH Treasury is continuing to grow and expand. A vacancy has arisen within the Treasury Operations team for a Corporate Treasury Analyst to join this highly successful team. The team is based in the Group’s head office in Stonemason’s Way, Rathfarnham, Dublin 16\. Due to the nature of this role the successful candidate will be required on\-site initially for training and thereafter working arrangements under our group hybrid policy. **Key Tasks And Responsibilities** The successful candidate will become a valuable member of the existing treasury operations/dealing team where the tasks and responsibilities below will be performed as part of the treasury operations team: * Take a leading role on Liquidity management across multi\-currency cash pools within the group. * Manage the Deposit book, commercial paper and other investment products and execution of the same. * Foreign exchange and commodity management and execution of hedges using derivative products: * Counterparty risk management monitoring and reporting. * Performing controls to monitor and report on the daily Treasury activities and processes. * Manage the process in conjunction with the Strategic projects team on Administration of internal funding arrangements. * Responsibility for the Treasury payments processing through the companies’ payments systems and banking portals. * Bank cash balance monitoring and reporting of cash held throughout the group (MT 940\). * Cash management and other banking duties. * Reporting daily and on an ad hoc basis as required on Treasury activities and processes. * Assisting and training junior members in the production of monthly KPI reporting to senior management. * Supporting various projects and initiatives in treasury operations (design, implementation and identification of process improvements). * Supporting the team through various SOX requirements, systems audits and internal audits throughout the year. **Education And Experience** * University degree in accounting/finance or professional accounting / treasury qualification (preferred) * A minimum of 3\+ years post qualification experience. * Must be professionally minded, motivated and a self\-starter keen to make a strong contribution to the Treasury team as a function within the Treasury department. * Ability to prioritise your tasks as part of a team plan and work to deadlines on a daily basis. * Can learn and apply controls and processes within tasks and systems. * Can understand complex situations and processes and explain them simply to senior management. * Work with people across internal functions, external banking relationships and Treasury system providers. * Sees opportunities and appreciates a drive for continuous improvement and best practices. This is an ideal opportunity to join a fast\-moving and dynamic function within the CRH Finance department. **What CRH Offers You** * A culture that values opportunity for growth, development, and internal promotion * Highly competitive salary package * Comprehensive secondary benefits * Significant contribution to your pension plan * Health and wellness programs, including an on\-site gym and fitness classes * Excellent opportunities to develop and progress with a global organization **Connect your future to CRH** We are curious to learn more about you. At CRH, we believe our mutual differences contribute to the healthy, productive, and enjoyable workspace we create. Please introduce yourself and send us your application. Is this role not for you, but do you know someone who would love to join the team? Please let us know! CRH finds it important that vacancies are shared to individuals that may find them interesting and/or could be suitable for the role Please contact our recruitment team at careers@crh.com. *CRH is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to creating an inclusive work environment for all employees and actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community.* *Benefits/perks listed above may vary depending on the nature of the employment with CRH and the country where you work.* ***Please note that we cannot accept any applications submitted through email for GDPR purposes. Candidates must apply through our job portal.*** ***We do not accept candidate introductions for this position from recruitment agencies, unless you have been instructed to do so by our recruitment team.***

title: Utilities Engineer
description: ****Utilities Engineer**** ****Location: Dunboyne, Co meath**** ****Duration: Permanent**** ****Hours: 39 Hours per week**** ****Overview of the role**** To provide Utilities Engineering expertise to the clients site, specifically for the major site systems e.g. HVAC, Pharmaceutical Water Systems Compressed Air, Gases, Steam etc. This role focuses on optimizing equipment and systems performance, improving reliability, driving continuous improvement, troubleshooting and Faultfinding. The job\-holder works as part of a multi\-functional Utilities team and will be expected to be flexible and agile in support of the group's overall mission. The role will include reviewing compliance documentation and Regulatory Audit attendance. ****Day to Day of the Role:**** * Become a reliability engineer for all site HVAC / Utility systems e.g. Chilled Water, HVAC, Steam and Boiler systems, Water storage and distribution and the site Fire Systems and GMP Clean Systems. * Gain a full understanding of the core utilities interdependencies and the criticality to all critical and GMP systems on site. * Generate reliability reports for these systems using alarm and event data from Building Management and Manufacturing Control Systems. * Work with site personnel to optimise the current alarm and event management strategy for site utilities systems. * Implement optimisation projects on all systems and ensure systems are operating at optimum e.g. \- Instrument replacement, calibration, range assessments etc. * Operate according to SOPs developed for site engineering and maintenance. * Take responsibility for all aspects of reliability projects on utilities. Ensure all reliability projects are executed in line with site Engineering standards and practices. * Management of audit action items generated from various site, corporate, cGMP and EHS audits. * Ensuring Engineering areas, in liaison with Contract Manager and Contract coordinator, facilities and equipment are kept in a clean, safe and serviceable condition, and general housekeeping high standards are maintained. ****Required Skills for the Role:**** * Minimum qualification Bsc or MSc/M.Eng Degree in Mechanical/Electrical or Building Services Engineering. * A good knowledge of Site Utilities Systems including HVAC, steam, water, gases, waste and Fire systems. * Excellent excel data manipulation and interpretation skills are essential to this role. * Excellent communication, interpersonal, and team\-working skills. * An ability to work independently and as a member of a team in a dynamic, fast\-paced environment * Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities **Please note:** ​If you are an EU/EEA national, you will be asked to show proof of right to work in Ireland. If you are a non\-EU/EEA national , you will require current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. Information on legislation and guides to the procedures in relation to obtaining greencard permits ,work permits, spousal/dependant permits is available on the Department of Jobs, Enterprise and Innovation website. We know how important it is to be fully committed to building and maintaining a diverse and inclusive place to work for every one of our colleagues. If you are a non\-EU/EEA national , you will require current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. title: Utilities Engineer
description: **Job Title –** Black Utilities Lead Technician **Location –** Onsite \- Blanchardstown **Type:** Permanent **Hours:** 39 Hours per week ( 07:30 to 16:00\) **Background:** We are the Ireland’s leading environmental services company. We provide a comprehensive range of energy, waste and water solutions and are dedicated to carbon reduction, protecting the environment and building the circular economy. We offer all employees a benefits package as standard which includes full VHI cover, pension and education assistance. When you see the world as we do, you see the chance to help the world take better care of its resources, and help it become a better place for everyone. It's why we're looking for someone who's just as committed as we are, to push for genuine change and bring our ambition of Ecological Transformation to life. We know that everyone here at can help us work alongside our communities, look after the environment, and contribute to our inclusive culture. **Overview of the role:** The Black Utilities Lead is responsible for the reliable operation, maintenance, and continuous improvement of all black utility systems supporting pharmaceutical manufacturing operations. The role ensures uninterrupted supply of critical utilities while maintaining full compliance with: cGMP requirements Regulatory standards, Site Quality Systems This position acts as the technical link between: * Site Management * Utilities Shift Teams * Engineering \& Maintenance * External Vendors The Black Utilities Lead also provides subject matter expertise in utility performance, troubleshooting, and lifecycle reliability. This role is critical in ensuring:Manufacturing continuity, Product quality protection, Regulatory compliance, Asset reliability. Failure of black utilities can directly impact pharmaceutical production, making this a key GMP\-supporting position. **Duties of the role include;** **Utilities Operations** * Ensure continuous operation of black utility systems including: * Steam * Condensate * Chilled Water * Cooling Water * Compressed Air * HVAC support systems * Monitor system performance and escalate risks to supply continuity * Act as technical lead for troubleshooting and fault resolution * Support commissioning and project activities when required **Maintenance \& Reliability** * Lead reactive maintenance to minimise production impact * Support development and optimisation of preventive maintenance programs * Ensure equipment is maintained in accordance with: cGMP, Site procedures, Engineering best practice * Identify critical spare parts and maintain appropriate MRO levels **Team Leadership** * Direct daily activities of Utilities Technicians * Coordinate work with: Site Manager, Utilities Coordinator, Clean Utilities Lead * Chair shift pass\-down meetings * Drive closure of operational actions **Continuous Improvement** * Apply continuous improvement methodologies to: Enhance system reliability, Improve energy efficiency, Reduce downtime risk * Collaborate with Engineering to introduce innovative solutions **Compliance** * Ensure all utility work aligns with:,cGMP, Change Control, Maintenance Procedures * Complete training in line with site training plans * Support audit readiness and inspection compliance **Stakeholder Coordination** * Act as primary contact for all black utilities\-related work * Coordinate vendor support activities * Attend weekly utilities planning meetings **Other duties include but not limited to;** * Support site projects * Assist commissioning activities * Provide cross\-functional technical support when required **Requirements for this role;** * Recognised **Trade Certificate** in one of the following: Mechanical Trade (Fitter / Pipefitter / Millwright) / Electrical Trade / Instrumentation Trade / Refrigeration / HVAC Trade *(Essential)* * Experience working in: Pharmaceutical / Biopharma / Regulated Manufacturing Environment *(Desirable)* * Strong technical knowledge of industrial utility systems * Ability to interpret P\&IDs and system schematics * Root cause analysis capability * Leadership and decision\-making skills * GMP awareness * Risk\-based maintenance approach

title: Veterinary Nurse - Small Animal
description: **Registered Veterinary Nurse – Small Animal** ---------------------------------------------- ***Anicare Vets, Santry – Dublin*** ----------------------------------- ***Full\-Time or Part\-Time \\u007C Permanent*** ------------------------------------------------ ***Up to €40,000 DOE \\u007C Flexible Working Available*** ---------------------------------------------------------- ***Graduates welcome \+ Nurse Academy Support*** ------------------------------------------------ **A Role Where You’re Truly Valued** ------------------------------------ If you’re looking for a nursing role where your skills are trusted, your growth is encouraged, and your work\-life balance genuinely matters — this could be the perfect next step. At Anicare Santry, you’ll join a friendly, close\-knit team where collaboration, support and high clinical standards go hand in hand. Whether you’re newly qualified or bring years of experience, you’ll be welcomed into an environment that allows you to develop at your own pace while feeling part of something bigger. **A Varied and Rewarding Nursing Role** --------------------------------------- This is a true general practice nursing position offering a great mix of hands\-on clinical work and client interaction. You’ll be actively involved in all aspects of patient care, from surgical support through to nurse consults, with the autonomy to use your skills fully. Your day\-to\-day will naturally include a blend of anaesthesia monitoring, assisting with routine procedures, running your own nurse consultations, and contributing to the smooth running of the practice, including stock and pharmacy management. There’s also the opportunity to be involved in inpatient care and occasionally support the front\-of\-house team when needed, giving you great exposure across all areas of practice life. **Designed Around You** ----------------------- We know flexibility matters, so this role is built with that in mind. title: Veterinary Practitioner - Small Animal
description: **Veterinary Practitioner \- Small Animal** ------------------------------------------- ***Anicare Vets, Clontarf Veterinary Hospital*** ------------------------------------------------ ***Full\-time, Permanent*** --------------------------- ***Salary up to €65,000 per annum DOE*** ---------------------------------------- ***Start date: October 2026 (flexible)*** ----------------------------------------- **Step Into a Role Where You Can Truly Make Your Mark** ------------------------------------------------------- If you're an experienced small animal vet looking for a role where your clinical judgement is trusted, your surgical skills are valued, and your voice genuinely matters — this could be exactly what you’ve been waiting for. At Anicare Clontarf, you'll join a well\-established, progressive practice that offers both stability and the opportunity to shape your role around your clinical interests. While we welcome a variety of interests, this position is best suited to a confident first\-opinion GP vet who enjoys a balanced caseload of consulting and surgery. **A Balanced, Varied GP Role** ------------------------------ This is a true first\-opinion role with an enjoyable mix of consultations and surgical work across the week. You’ll typically spend 1–2 days in surgery and 2–3 days consulting, managing a varied and interesting caseload. The role offers a sustainable and structured schedule, designed to support both clinical quality and work\-life balance: * 36\.5 hours per week * 1 in 6 weekends (approx. 1\.5 per cycle) * No out\-of\-hours or on\-call responsibilities * Shared bank holiday rota (1–2 per year) This is an ideal opportunity for a vet who is confident working with a team of experienced professionals, happy managing their own caseload, and comfortable contributing to the daily rhythm of a busy GP practice. **A Supportive, Experienced Team** ---------------------------------- You’ll be part of a friendly and collaborative team of four vets, four nurses, and three support staff, all working together in a positive, well\-run environment. The practice itself is modern and well\-equipped, with digital radiography, orthopaedic equipment, and a loyal, long\-standing client base that allows for high\-quality, continuity\-driven care. This role is suited to an experienced vet who can work with confidence and autonomy — it’s not designed for new graduates or those requiring close day\-to\-day mentoring. **Part of a Trusted Dublin Network** ------------------------------------ Anicare is one of Dublin’s longest\-established veterinary groups, with clinics across Clontarf, Santry, Blanchardstown and Glasnevin. You’ll benefit from access to a wider network of experienced colleagues, including certificate holders, enabling collaboration, case discussion and internal referrals — all while maintaining the close\-knit feel of your home clinic. **What We’re Looking For** -------------------------- We’re looking for a VCI\-registered Veterinary Surgeon who: * Has at least a few years’ experience in small animal general practice * Is confident in both consultations and routine surgery (including dentals) * Can work independently and manage their own caseload effectively * Brings a proactive, reliable and team\-oriented approach Applicants must be eligible to work in Ireland (please note that visa sponsorship is not available for this role). **A Package That Supports You Professionally and Personally** ------------------------------------------------------------- Alongside a competitive salary, you’ll benefit from a comprehensive and well\-rounded package designed to support your wellbeing, development and long\-term career: ### **Work\-Life Balance \& Wellbeing** 22 days annual leave plus birthday leave No out\-of\-hours or Sunday work Paid maternity, paternity, and adoptive leave packages Cycle to Work scheme Employee Assistance Programme and Wellbeing Centre Company sick pay and paid bereavement leave ### **Development \& Career Growth** €1,300 annual CPD allowance with 5 days paid CPD leave (pro\-rata) Fully funded certificates, diplomas, or PhDs Career progression opportunities, clinical and non\-clinical Access to internal mentorship and development programmes ### **Financial \& Professional Support** VCI membership and VDS cover paid Annual pay reviews Employer pension contributions Dedicated CARE fund – €1,100 annual contribution per vet to use on a case of your choice Discounted veterinary fees across the IVC Evidensia network Pawesome Benefits – retail discounts and cashback options **Is This Your Next Step?** --------------------------- If you’re looking for a role that combines clinical variety, autonomy, and a genuinely supportive environment — with the flexibility to shape your focus and grow your skills — Anicare Clontarf could be the perfect fit. We’d love to have a conversation and explore how this opportunity could work for you. Up to €65,000 DOE We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. title: Videographer
description: **About the Role** ------------------ At Thérapie Clinic, we’ve been enhancing confidence and empowering lives for over 20 years. With more than 85 clinics across the UK, Ireland, and the USA, we’re proud to be Europe’s No.1 provider of Aesthetic Treatments. Our mission is simple: to make advanced, effective treatments accessible to everyone. Enjoy a fulfilling and rewarding career with opportunities for growth, development, and long\-term success. Join us and Do Your Best Work at Thérapie! As we continue to scale rapidly, we are looking for a talented and experienced **Videographer to** join us. This role offers the opportunity to shape the future of our workforce. The **Videographer** rolewill join the Marketing Team based in Dublin. As someone who lives and breathes creativity, understands social media trends, and has the skills to bring ideas to life through engaging video and photography content, you’ll be responsible for producing scroll\-stopping content, working closely with our design and performance teams to deliver high\-impact creative including Long format Video, Short Format Reels and Photography assets for Thérapie Clinic. **Key Responsibilities** ------------------------ * Plan, shoot, and edit video and photo content tailored for social media platforms and our websites. * Long\-form content that tells a deeper story. For example: Explaining complex treatment services, Immersive Brand Storytelling \& Testimonials, Educational \& Authority\-Building Content, Comprehensive Product Education \& Conversion or guiding viewers toward a decision. * Short Format Reels/videos: Hook\-led, visually engaging creative videos for social media. Knowledge and experience with social media trends and visual hooks vital. Must speak the language of reels. Edit and repurpose longer videos into shorter clips * Work closely and proactively with design and performance teams to align content with campaign goals, brand standards, and in\-clinic execution. * Video editing/ photo retouching * Collaborate with the design team to deliver polished, on\-brand and elevated UGC feel content. * Take ownership of content projects from concept to delivery * Proactively pitch new ideas and content formats * Create content that drives engagement, reach, and conversions across social platforms, delivering content aligned with performance marketing goals. * Stay up\-to\-date with trends and techniques in video and photography for social media. * Travel to various locations across Ireland and the UK. * Full, clean driving licence preferred **Required Qualifications and Skills:** * Proven experience in video production (filming, lighting, audio capture). * Expertise in **Adobe Premiere Pro** or **Final Cut Pro**. * Proficiency in **CapCut** for mobile and social media editing. * Experience in both short\-form (Reels/TikTok) and long\-form video editing. * Skilled in storytelling and storyboarding techniques. * Experience in video color grading. * Strong photography skills, including camera handling and lighting. * Basic photo editing/retouching proficiency in **Adobe Creative Suite** (Photoshop/Lightroom). **Preferred Qualifications (Nice to Haves)** * Experience with **Adobe After Effects** for motion graphics. * Experience creating graphics in **Canva**. **Qualifications and Skills** ----------------------------- * Ideal for a recent graduate with a strong portfolio * 1–3 years’ experience (including freelance, internships, or personal projects)os (experience with tools like Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or similar is a plus). * Strong understanding of short\-form, social\-first content (Reels, TikTok, Shorts) * Proven experience in storyboarding, scripting, filming, and editing video content. * Demonstrable experience in photography. * Comfortable shooting both high\-end and mobile\-first content * Brings creativity, energy, and adaptability to every project. * Is highly organised and comfortable working on multiple projects at once. * What matters most is your skill, eye for detail, and passion for content creation. **Over to the best part, the Perks!** ------------------------------------- * #### **Uncapped Earnings** With our uncapped commission structure, the sky's the limit! * #### **Treat Yourself** Enjoy free laser hair removal, Free EmSella and generous discounts on our skin, AWI and body treatments – because our skin maestros deserve the best. * #### **Share the Love** Avail spouse and family discounts, spreading the joy of Thérapie. * #### **Sisterly Love** 10% off Thérapie Fertility \& Optilase treatments – because family helps family. * #### **Thérapie Fertility** We offer one free treatment cycle of IVF/ICSI/RIVF to all team members after they have been with us for 2 years. * #### **Optilase Eye Clinic** We offer free laser eye surgery to all team members after they have been with us for 2 years. * #### **Skin Royalty** Discounts on leading skin brands \& treatments (Murad, Skin Theory). * #### **Career Pathways** Embark on a journey of career progression \& management training – we believe in promoting from within! * #### **Knowledge Oasis** Immerse yourself in a full week of paid training in our academy, where knowledge is power. * #### **Big Birthday Bash Leave** Enjoy an extra day off on your landmark birthdays to celebrate in style! * #### **Longevity Bonus** Earn extra annual leave days after 2 years of dedicated service. * #### **Employee Assistance Scheme** Access confidential support services for personal and professional well\-being. **Application Process** ----------------------- STEP 1 **Explore Opportunities****:** Start by finding the role that excites you. STEP 2 **Submit Your Application****:** Apply by uploading your CV. STEP 3 **Application Review****:** Our Talent Acquisition Partners will carefully review your application. STEP 4 **Initial Contact****:** If shortlisted, our Talent Acquisition Team will reach out to schedule an interview. STEP 5 **Interview Stage****:** Complete the interview process with our team. STEP 6 **Offer \& Onboarding****:** If successful, you'll receive your offer along with onboarding details. STEP 7 **Begin Your Journey****:** Attend our state\-of\-the\-art training facility to kickstart your career with us.

title: Warehouse Operative (Temporary)
description: Looking for a practical, hands\-on role in a friendly, organised team? We’re a small warehouse team at **Vox Tech Ireland,** part of **Vox Group**, a global leader in tour guide audio technology. As we head into our busy season, we’re looking to welcome **two Warehouse Operatives** to support our day\-to\-day operations. **No previous experience needed.** The position is **full\-time, Monday to Friday from 8:00am to 4:30pm**, and is **temporary until 31 October 2026**. **What you’ll be doing** * Scanning and checking returned equipment * Inspecting, cleaning, and repackaging devices * Picking and packing orders accurately * Testing audio equipment and preparing it for dispatch * Applying shipping labels and preparing orders for collection * Flagging missing or damaged items to the team **What we’re looking for** * No previous experience required * **Strong attention to detail (essential)** * Good level of English * Reliable, organised, and comfortable following clear instructions * Full work rights in Ireland (no visa restrictions) **Trial period** * The role includes a **one\-week paid trial** Job Types: Full\-time, Fixed term Contract length: 7 months Pay: €14\.15 per hour Work Location: In person

title: Warehouse-Based Commercial Equipment Cleaner
description: PLEASE NOTE: ONLY APPLICATIONS THAT SATISFY BASIC REQUIREMENTS WILL BE REVIEWED We are a refrigeration and catering equipment rental company seeking an experienced Commercial Equipment Cleaner who will be responsible for the deep cleaning, decontamination, and technical cleaning of commercial refrigeration and industrial catering equipment,. This role ensures all equipment is returned to a sanitized, fully functional, and presentable state for re\-hire or usage, while maintaining high hygiene standards within the warehouse facility. Key Responsibilities **1\. Equipment Cleaning \& Maintenance** * **Deep Cleaning:** Clean commercial equipment (e.g., catering, hospitality, refrigeration units) using equipment provided including high pressure hoses and compressors * **Decontamination:** Follow strict sanitization protocols to disinfect equipment to food\-grade or high\-industry standards. * **Testing/Inspection:** Perform basic functional checks, rinsing, flushing, and testing of equipment to ensure it is working properly before being returned to stock. * **Component Care:** Clean specialized internal components of machinery to remove buildup, ensuring operational efficiency. **2\. Warehouse Sanitation \& Safety** * **Floor Maintenance:** Maintain warehouse safety and cleanliness. * **Waste Handling:** Manage waste disposal, including operating cardboard balers, emptying bins, and handling hazardous materials safely if required. * **Facility Cleaning:** Clean racking, loading docks, walls, and high\-touch areas (door handles, handrails) **3\. Safety \& Compliance** * **Health \& Safety:** Adhere strictly to Health and Safety regulations, including wearing appropriate Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) at all times. * **Chemical Handling:** Safely handle and mix cleaning chemicals, following COSHH (Control of Substances Hazardous to Health) standards. * **Reporting:** Log daily activities, report maintenance issues or damaged equipment to the warehouse manager immediately. Requirements \& Qualifications * **Experience:** Previous experience as an industrial cleaner, warehouse operative, or commercial equipment cleaner is highly preferred. * **Physical Stamina:** Ability to perform heavy manual work, including lifting, bending, and standing for long periods. * **Safety Focus:** Strong awareness of health and safety standards. * **Reliability:** Trustworthy, punctual, and able to work on own initiative. * **Technical Skills:** Ability to work with industrial machinery (e.g., pressure washers) and use a smartphone for task reporting. * **Working Conditions** * Warehouse/Industrial setting. * May involve exposure to cleaning chemicals, noise, and wet conditions. * Flexible hours, including weekends or evenings, may be required. Pay: €14\.15\-€15\.75 per hour Benefits: * Company pension * On\-site parking * Sick pay Education: * Leaving Certificate (required) Experience: * Industrial cleaning: 2 years (required) Language: * Excellent Spoken and Written English (required) Licence/Certification: * Manual Handling (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person
